subject: Product Management
title: Stakeholder Management
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Stakeholder Management.

hook.text: Sales wants more features. Engineering wants less scope. Executives want faster delivery. The PM is in the middle.

buildup.text: Stakeholders are anyone with influence over or interest in the product: execs, sales, support, legal, partners.

discovery.text: The key skill is translating: turning engineering constraints into business language and vice versa.

twist.text: PMs who try to please everyone please no one. Saying no is as important as saying yes.

climax.text: Great stakeholder management means everyone feels heard — even when the answer isn't what they wanted.

quiz.question: What is the key to effective stakeholder management?
options: Translating between groups and ensuring everyone feels heard, even when saying no
Always agreeing with the loudest voice
